Article Title: Amit Shah calls on president amid buzz of reshuffle of union council of ministers
Article Text:
Amit Shah calls on president amid buzz of reshuffle of union council of ministers

NeEW DELHI: Union Home Minister Amit Shah on Thursday called on President Droupadi Murmu at the Rashtrapati Bhavan here amid the buzz over a reshuffle of the union council of ministers.The president's office shared a picture of the meeting in a post on X."Union Minister for Home Affairs and Cooperation Shri Amit Shah called on President Droupadi Murmu at Rashtrapati Bhavan," the post said.On Tuesday, Prime Minister Narendra Modi had called on President Murmu.The meetings assume significance given the speculation that a reshuffle of the union council of ministers is imminent.Veteran BJP leader from Kerala George Kurian recently resigned from the council of ministers after his Rajya Sabha termed expired. The 65-year-old was Union minister of state for minority affairs and also for fisheries, animal husbandry and dairying.The BJP decided not to renominate Kurian, whose term in the Upper House ended on June 21.Besides, Minister of State for Railways Ravneet Singh, whose Rajya Sabha term also ended on June 21, has not been renominated by the BJP to the Upper House either.Singh, who had been a Rajya Sabha member from Rajasthan since August 2024, also holds charge as the minister of state for food processing industries.A few Union ministers have been given key organisational responsibilities in their home states. Their appointment fuelled the buzz of a possible reshuffle.BJP national president Nitin Nabin on May 28 appointed Union Minister of State for Road Transport and Highways and Corporate Affairs Harsh Malhotra as Delhi BJP chief. In December last year, Union Minister of State for Finance Pankaj Chaudhary was the new president of the BJP's Uttar Pradesh unit.
Article Summary: Amit Shah calls on president amid buzz of reshuffle of union council of ministers. Prime Minister Narendra Modi had called on President Droupadi Murmu on Tuesday. The meetings assume significance given the speculation that a reshuffle is imminent.Veteran BJP leader from Kerala George Kurian recently resigned from the council.

Article Title: 'AAP fabricated forensic report': Raghav Chadha demands Punjab CM Bhagwant Mann's resignation in video row
Article Text:
Raghav Chadha demands Punjab CM Bhagwant Mann's resignation in video row

NEW DELHI: Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) MP in Rajya Sabha Raghav Chadha has demanded the resignation of Punjab chief minister Bhagwant Mann over a purported video which created political uproar for hurting sentiments of the Sikh, accusing the Aam Aadmi Party (AAP) government of fabricating a forensic report to cover up the incident.Addressing the controversy, Chadha claimed that any doubts over the authenticity of the video had now been dispelled. "Those who had even a slight doubt about whether this desecration video was real, AI-generated or fake have now been cleared of all doubts and suspicions," he said."Bhagwant Mann and the Aam Aadmi Party had to fabricate a forensic report to conceal the actual desecration video...The entire Sikh community, the Guru Nanak Naamleva community, is hurt, angry, and enraged today," he added.Claiming that the issue had deeply hurt religious sentiments, Chadha said the Sikh community and followers of Guru Nanak were "hurt, angry and enraged" over the matter.He also called for a probe into the circumstances under which the forensic report was obtained. According to Chadha, investigators should examine which officials were involved, whether public funds were misused, and if any financial irregularities, including possible money laundering, were linked to the report."The entire Sikh community, the Guru Nanak Naamleva community, is hurt, angry, and enraged today...An investigation should also be conducted into how this fabricated forensic report was procured. Which officers were involved? Which government money or private money was used to bribe this report? Does this also constitute money laundering?" he said."Misuse of government funds. All these things are now under investigation, and investigation and action must be taken on this matter...Punjab CM Bhagwant Mann should resign. Under the new sacrilege law, the first FIR and the first prosecution should be against Bhagwant Mann himself," he added.The controversy centres on a purported video that allegedly shows Punjab CM Bhagwant Mann engaging in conduct considered disrespectful towards Sikh Gurus. While AAP has maintained that the video is either manipulated or taken out of context, the opposition BJP and Sikh religious bodies have accused Mann of sacrilege and hurting Sikh sentiments.The row intensified after Gurugram Police began investigating allegations that a forensic report, which reportedly sought to discredit the video, was procured through a Rs 10 lakh payment. BJP leaders have alleged that there was an attempt to suppress the controversy using money and state machinery, charges denied by AAP.The issue also reached the Akal Takht, the highest temporal seat of Sikh authority, which summoned Mann and took action in connection with the alleged incident. The BJP has since demanded legal action against the Punjab chief minister, accusing him of disrespecting Sikh religious sentiments and attempting to cover up the matter.
Article Summary: BJP MP in Rajya Sabha Raghav Chadha demands resignation of Punjab chief minister Bhagwant Mann. Chadha accuses Aam Aadmi Party government of fabricating a forensic report to cover up the incident. Claims that any doubts over the authenticity of the video had now been dispelled.

Article Title: Ram Mandir embezzlement row: SC declines urgent hearing on probe plea; asks petitioner to return on June 29
Article Text:
Supreme Court, Ram Mandir (File photo)

NEW DELHI: The Supreme Court on Thursday refused to entertain an oral request for the urgent listing of a petition seeking a court-monitored investigation into alleged financial irregularities in the Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust in Ayodhya.The court asked the petitioner's counsel to mention the matter on June 29 for listing.The plea seeks a court-monitored investigation into the alleged financial irregularities in the Trust, including the registration of an FIR and the constitution of a Special Investigation Team under the Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI). It also seeks directions to preserve all physical, digital and financial records, including donation registers, audit reports, CCTV footage and bank records.The development comes days after a three-member Special Investigation Team (SIT), constituted by the Uttar Pradesh government, submitted its preliminary report on alleged embezzlement of donations at the Ram temple.The SIT handed over its report to Additional Chief Secretary (Home) Sanjay Prasad after completing a week-long inquiry into the handling of temple donations and financial management systems. According to officials, the report recommends administrative reforms, including the appointment of a chief executive officer on the lines of the management model followed at the Kashi Vishwanath Temple to improve transparency, accountability and administrative efficiency.The Uttar Pradesh government had constituted the SIT on June 13 after the temple trust itself sought a probe into allegations of theft and misappropriation of devotees' offerings. The three-member panel is headed by Lucknow Divisional Commissioner Vijay Vishwas Pant and includes Inspector General Kiran S and Special Secretary (Finance) Neel Ratan.During the investigation, the SIT examined donation collection and counting procedures, scrutinised financial records and recorded statements of nearly 150 people associated with the temple administration. Officials said the preliminary report contains recommendations aimed at strengthening oversight mechanisms and improving the management of donations. The contents of the report have not been made public.The controversy has also sparked political reactions. Former Chhattisgarh chief minister Bhupesh Baghel accused the BJP and its affiliates of "looting in the name of Ram", while Samajwadi Party chief Akhilesh Yadav alleged attempts to "cover up" the matter. Senior Congress leader Digvijaya Singh also expressed concern over the alleged financial irregularities.The allegations surfaced after claims of missing donation funds and financial mismanagement at the Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust. The SIT is also examining allegations related to cash theft, missing ornaments donated by devotees, administrative practices and disputed land transactions. The state government is expected to examine the preliminary findings before deciding on further action.
Article Summary: Supreme Court refuses to entertain oral request for urgent listing of petition. Petition seeks a court-monitored investigation into alleged financial irregularities in the Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust. Court asked the petitioner's counsel to mention the matter on June 29 for listing.

Article Title: BJP accuses Kharges of land grab, rejects charges against MP CM Mohan Yadav
Article Text: New Delhi: BJP Wednesday accused Congress president Mallikarjun Kharge and his son Priyank, a Karnataka minister, of “land grab” in their home state for ostensible purposes as diverse as setting up of ventures in aerospace and defence and promotion of the Pali language.It is the Congress dispensation in the state that has enriched them through their family trust, BJP alleged, marking an apparent distinction with the charges against Madhya Pradesh CM Mohan Yadav’s extended family members allegedly purchasing several chunks of private land since he took office in 2023. It also said, “Public land that was earlier (Karnataka) govt’s and public property was handed over to Mallikarjun Kharge’s private trust. ”Stepping up its attack, Congress accused BJP and RSS of patronising corruption, while seeking a SC inquiry into the MP issue. MP Congress chief Jitu Patwari said there are many instances where powerful people in the state have bought land in areas where new roads and highways are to be constructed.Daring the Congress govt in Karnataka to take action against the Kharges, BJP said, “Mallikarjun Kharge and Priyank Kharge have created a new modus operandi of land grab in Karnataka under the name of Siddharth Vihara Trust, which reminds of the Gandhi-Vadra family’s land grab.”The party has dismissed the charges against Mohan Yadav as baseless.BJP spokesperson Pradeep Bhandari said, “Valuable lands were given to the trust, whose members also include Congress president’s wife and son-in-law, in Bengaluru, Gulbarga, and Banashankari.” He added, “Did Mallikarjun Kharge use his power and influence? Did Priyank Kharge use his power and influence? Was Karnataka’s five acres of industrial land, worth Rs 100 crore, privately grabbed.” In 2024, Karnataka Industrial Area Development Board allotted five acres of land to the trust for the purpose of undertaking research and development in aerospace and defence sectors even though it has no link with the field, he said.
Article Summary: BJP accuses Congress president Mallikarjun Kharge and his son Priyank, a Karnataka minister, of ‘land grab’ in their home state. It is the Congress dispensation in the state that has enriched them through their family trust, BJP alleged. The party has dismissed the charges against Mohan Yadav as baseless.

Article Title: BJP demands FIR against Punjab CM Bhagwant Mann under new law
Article Text:
Mann

Manjinder Singh Sirsa

NEW DELHI: BJP on Wednesday demanded an FIR against Punjab CM Bhagwant Mann and some state govt officials under the state’s newly enacted anti-sacrilege law as it showed purported visuals and text messages involving the accused, including cops, in the case of fabrication of a forensic report to reject a video allegedly showing Mann defiling Sikh gurus as fake.BJP likened the AAP govt in the state to the Mughals for belittling the sentiments of Sikhs. The party has seized on the edict of Akal Takht, the highest authority in Sikhism, against Mann to sharpen its attack on Punjab govt ahead of the assembly election in the state early next year.The Akal Takht had dubbed the video authentic after getting its own forensic reports and called the CM “guru dokhi” (anti-guru) and “Khalsa panth virodhi” (anti-Khalsa panth). Delhi govt ministersaid Punjab police officers and persons linked with a private laboratory had colluded to produce a report declaring the video fake in what was an attempt to undermine the authority of Akal Takht. The BJP neta said inquiries pointed to the involvement of Ludhiana commissioner Swapan Sharma and SP Jashandeep Singh in the episode. Sirsa said as the investigation progressed, it emerged “Mann’s blue-eyed boy, Sharma, and SP Jashandeep Singh, through a person named Jaspreet Singh in Haryana, arranged a meeting at a hotel in Gurugram with a forensic laboratory”.“Money (Rs 10 lakh) was exchanged during the meeting, and the video was heavily edited to make it appear as if Mann was not present in it and the video was fake.”
Article Summary: BJP likened the AAP govt in the state to the Mughals for belittling the sentiments of Sikhs. The party has seized on the edict of Akal Takht, the highest authority in Sikhism, against Mann.

Article Title: 'Not related to him': Rahul Gandhi expresses regret in defamation case filed by Shivraj Singh Chouhan's son
Article Text:
Rahul Gandhi (File Photo)





BHOPAL: Congress MP and Leader of Opposition in Lok Sabha Rahul Gandhi has approached the Madhya Pradesh high court, expressing regret over his alleged defamatory statement involving union minister Shivraj Singh Chouhan ’s son Kartikeya Singh.The application was filed by Rahul's lawyer on Wednesday, stating that the remark made by the Congress leader was not related to Singh.Kartikeya Singh had filed a defamation complaint against the Leader of Opposition in the Lok Sabha before a Bhopal court, alleging that Gandhi’s statement had harmed his reputation.In his complaint filed before the court handling cases against MPs and MLAs, Singh alleged that during an election rally in Jhabua in 2018, Gandhi had referred to his name while speaking about the Panama Papers leak scandal.Following the complaint, the court issued a summons directing Gandhi to appear personally. The Congress leader then moved the High Court seeking to quash the summons and the defamation proceedings.Justice Pramod Kumar Agarwal will hear Gandhi’s petition on Thursday.During Wednesday’s hearing, the petitioner produced records of the lower court as directed by the high court. Advocate Sankalp Kochhar appeared for the complainant before the high court.
Article Summary: Kartikeya Singh had filed a defamation complaint against the Leader of Opposition in the Lok Sabha before a Bhopal court. Singh alleged that during an election rally in Jhabua in 2018, Gandhi had referred to his name while speaking about the Panama Papers leak scandal.

Article Title: Patil vs Rajenimbalkar: Dharashiv funding credit war heats up
Article Text:
Ranajagjitsinha Patil

Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar:





A day after the state urban development department, led by deputy CM Eknath Shinde , sanctioned Rs 18 crore for the Dharashiv Municipal Council, MP Omprakash Rajenimbalkar, who recently switched to Shiv Sena, and Tuljapur BJP MLA Ranajagjitsinha Patil both claimed credit for securing the funds.The development comes amid the long-standing family rivalry between the two politicians, now extending into a second generation, and continuing despite both being part of Mahayuti.Rajenimbalkar said he had been consistently pursuing the demand for funds for Dharashiv’s development. He thanked chief minister Devendra Fadnavis, deputy CM Sunetra Pawar, and particularly deputy CM Shinde for initiating what he called a “new chapter” in the city’s growth.Patil described the sanction as the outcome of sustained follow-up efforts, saying, “Persistent efforts for the all-round development of Dharashiv city have yielded significant success, with the approval of Rs 18 crore under key state schemes. Mahayuti govt remains committed to the city’s progress and this fund will provide strong momentum for future development.”The rivalry between the two families dates back to 2006, when Rajenimbalkar’s father, Pawanraje, was murdered. Patil’s father, Padamsinh Patil, was named as an accused in the case but was recently acquitted by a special CBI court. Following Rajenimbalkar’s switch to Shinde’s faction on June 21, Patil had expressed hope that the long-standing feud would come to an end. Rajenimbalkar, however, made it clear that political alignment would not erase the rivalry. “Irrespective of party affiliations, I will not hesitate to oppose such mentalities,” he said.Responding to this, Patil declined to escalate the matter, stating he would comment at an appropriate time.
Article Summary: MP Omprakash Rajenimbalkar and Tuljapur BJP MLA Ranajagjitsinha Patil both claimed credit for securing the funds. The development comes amid the long- standing family rivalry between the two politicians, now extending into a second generation.

Article Title: Marathwada MLC polls deliver expected wins to Mahayuti allies
Article Text:
BJP workers celebrating victory in the MLC poll near the vote counting centre in Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar

Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar:





The Marathwada local authorities’ elections largely followed expected lines, with BJP and Shiv Sena winning three and one seats, respectively, but suspected cross-voting from the MVA camp significantly boosted Mahayuti’s victory margins in three of the four constituencies.In the Aurangabad-Jalna constituency, BJP’s Suhas Shirsath secured a decisive win over Shiv Sena (UBT) candidate Ganesh Lokhande, polling 455 votes — reportedly exceeding the NDA ’s estimated strength — and winning by a margin of 320 votes.“I was confident of getting more than 450 votes. I would work for the cause of people and strengthen my party further,” said Shirsath, a close associate of Rajasthan Governor Haribhau Bagade.A similar pattern played out in Nanded, where BJP’s sitting MLC Amar Rajurkar defeated MVA-backed Krushna Patil by 255 votes amid claims of cross-voting in favour of Mahayuti. The contest had drawn attention against the backdrop of ‘Operation Tiger’, with Patil being the son of Sena (UBT) MP Nagesh Patil Ashtikar.The most striking result came from Osmanabad–Latur–Beed, where BJP’s Basavraj Patil registered a record 719-vote victory margin, the highest in Maharashtra.The landslide is widely attributed to large-scale cross-voting, reportedly influenced by rebel Sena (UBT) MP Omprakash Rajenimbalkar and others.In contrast, the Parbhani–Hingoli seat saw a relatively close contest, with Shiv Sena’s Saeed Khan defeating Sena (UBT) nominee Vivek Navandar by just 83 votes. Here, suspected cross-voting from the NCP camp is believed to have aided MVA, limiting the winning margin.Despite the setbacks, MVA politicians framed the contest as a principled fight rather than a numbers game. “We knew we did not have the magic figure of voters with us. Still, our fight was for democracy and to protect its democratic principles,” said Congress general secretary Jitendra Dehade.
Article Summary: The Marathwada local authorities’ elections largely followed expected lines, with BJP and Shiv Sena winning three and one seats, respectively. But suspected cross-voting from the MVA camp significantly boosted Mahayuti’s victory margins in three of the four constituencies.

Article Title: ​​Rebel Sena (UBT) MP’s son loses Nanded MLC poll, suspended from party
Article Text:
Krushna Patil

Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar:





MVA-backed candidate Krushna Patil, who is the son of rebel Hingoli Shiv Sena (UBT) MP Nagesh Patil Ashitikar, lost the Nanded local authorities’ constituency poll to BJP ’s Amar Rajurkar by 255 votes. Soon after the results were announced, Sena (UBT) MP Sanjay Raut announced that Krushna Patil has been expelled for his anti-party activities.Patil secured only 84 votes while Rajurkar garnered 339 votes.The MLC poll for Nanded was held under the shadow of “Operation Tiger” that eventually, on Monday, saw the Hingoli Sena (UBT) MP leaving the Uddhav Thackeray faction to join Shiv Sena-led by deputy chief minister Eknath Shinde.Speaking to the reporters after the defeat, Krsuhna Patil said he would not divulge his future political path at present. He also said that Sena (UBT) had set irrational expectations for him to prove his loyalty to MVA.He was referring to comments by Sena (UBT) deputy leader Sushama Andhare, who had asked Patil to perform symbolic last rites of his father and also organise ‘jode maro’ (hit with shoes) agitation against Ashtikar to prove his loyalty. Andhare’s comments had sparked a war of words with Patil terming her “mentally imbalanced”.“Now, I will offer a befitting welcome to my father (after his return from Mumbai). Entire Maharashtra should come to Nanded airport to witness it,” Patil said.Congress was keen to contest the MLC polls in Nanded with its candidate Ramdas Patil filing the nomination. He, however, was asked to withdraw his nomination to make way for Patil.Notably, while Sena (UBT) had virtually disowned Patil in the wake of his father rebelling against Thackeray, the local unit of Congress had issued a whip asking its voters to vote for the MVA candidate.
Article Summary: MVA-backed candidate Krushna Patil lost the Nanded local authorities’ constituency poll to BJP’s Amar Rajurkar by 255 votes. Patil is the son of rebel Hingoli Shiv Sena (UBT) MP Nagesh Patil Ashitikar. The MLC poll for Nanded was held under the shadow of ‘Operation Tiger’ that eventually saw Patil leave the Uddhav Thackeray faction.

Article Title: Teen who rammed SUV into woman before crashing into 6 vehicles in Navi Mumbai booked for drunk driving
Article Text:
Drunk teen SUV driver drags woman 50m, hits 6 parked vehicles

Watch CCTV Captures Delhi Teen’s Speeding SUV Seconds Before Fatal Dwarka Crash CCTV Captures Delhi Teen’s Speeding SUV Seconds Before Fatal Dwarka Crash

NAVI MUMBAI: The CBD-Belapur police have invoked section 185 of the Motor Vehicle Act for drunk driving against the accused 19-year-old SUV driver.The teenager had hit and dragged a woman pedestrian for 50 metres and caused her severe injuries and then rammed into six vehicles parked on the roadside in Artist village, sector-8 on Friday.Arun Pawar, senior inspector of CBD-Belapur said, the accused SUV driver Vikram Kamble's medical test report received from NMMC hospital, Nerul confirmed that he was under the influence of alcohol while driving.Subsequently, relevant section 185 Of Motor Vehicle Act that penalizes drunk driving were invoked against him and will be mentioned in the chargesheet that will be submitted in the court.The woman Mohini Shinde (35), who worked as househelp, sustained severe head injuries and bruises on her face and near the knees is recuperating at MGM hospital, CBD-Belapur and is out of danger, informed doctors.The accident took place in the residential locality of Artist village in Sector-8, CBD-Belapur around 5.30 pm on Friday. Police said the speeding SUV hit the woman from behind while she was walking close to the pavement and dragged her for nearly 50 metres before crashing into six vehicles parked along the roadside.Local residents rushed the injured woman to MGM Hospital, where she received prompt treatment. The incident was also captured on CCTV cameras installed in the area.Police said the SUV driver Vikram Gangadhar Kamble (19) was accompanied by his relative Akash Chandrakant Kamble (26), a private chauffeur. Both were detained by locals at the spot before the police arrived, following which an FIR was registered for rash and negligent driving and damage to property.
Article Summary: The CBD-Belapur police have invoked section 185 of the Motor Vehicle Act for drunk driving against the accused 19-year-old SUV driver. The teenager had hit and dragged a woman pedestrian for 50 metres and caused her severe injuries and then rammed into six vehicles parked on the roadside. The woman Mohini Shinde (35), who worked as househelp, sustained severe head injuries and bruises on her face and near the knees.

Article Title: 16-year-old girl gang-raped by two men aged over 60 near Mumbai
Article Text:
(The victim's identity has not been revealed to protect her privacy as per Supreme court directives on cases related to sexual assault)





THANE: A 16-year-old girl was allegedly raped by two senior citizens in Maharashtra's Thane district, police said on Sunday.The accused, both above 60 years of age, were arrested in connection with the attack, which occurred in a slum colony in Netivali village on Saturday night.According to police, the duo allegedly caught hold of the girl and raped her repeatedly.The survivor managed to escape their clutches and lodged a complaint with the police.The accused have been booked under the relevant provisions of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita and the Protection of Children from Sexual Offences (POCSO) Act.
Article Summary: A 16-year-old girl was allegedly raped by two senior citizens in Maharashtra's Thane district. The accused, both above 60 years of age, were arrested in connection with the attack. The survivor managed to escape their clutches and lodged a complaint with the police.

Article Title: Nashik MLC polls winner Gokul Gite joins Shiv Sena as associate member
Article Text:
Gite secured 357 votes in the election, defeating Darade by 109 votes and comfortably crossing the winning quota of 303.

Nashik

:

TOI





Hours after defeating Shiv Sena ’s official candidate Narendra Darade in the Nashik local authorities election, independent candidate Gokul Gite joined Shiv Sena as an associate member in the legislative council.The swift political move raised eyebrows among Shiv Sena and BJP politicians, while Darade claimed that deputy chief minister Eknath Shinde was well aware of how the party’s candidate was treated by BJP during the contest.“Even before the election results were officially declared, a close associate of Gokul Gite received a call from a Shiv Sena minister asking him to come to Mumbai by a chartered flight,” said a Shiv Sena member backing Darade. “Though the minister initially denied it, claiming such a quick move was impossible, he eventually flew to Nashik himself to bring Gite to Mumbai by plane. It strongly suggests that the entire sequence of events was pre-planned,” he added.A BJP politician also expressed surprise at the development. “On June 9, three ministers — Girish Mahajan, Uday Samant and Dada Bhuse — were persuading Gite to pull out of the race.Suddenly, after the results are out, MLC Gokul Gite, a close confidante of Girish Mahajan, is in Shiv Sena. This surprises everybody,” he said.Gite secured 357 votes in the election, defeating Darade by 109 votes and comfortably crossing the winning quota of 303.Darade, however, appeared to play it safe when asked if he shared these suspicions. “We were ready to contest the election straight forward manner, but were told it would go against the interests of Mahayuti. We were betrayed, and Shiv Sena chief and deputy CM Eknath Shinde is well aware of the situation, as well as how our alliance partner (BJP) treated Shiv Sena during the election,” he toldOn Gite’s sudden induction, Darade said he was informed by Shinde himself. “I was in Mumbai around 4pm on Monday when I received a call from deputy CM Shinde. He explained that it was crucial to strengthen the party’s presence in the legislative council and expand our footprint in Nashik district, which is why Gokul Gite was being brought in. I told him I had no objections,” he said.
Article Summary: Independent candidate Gokul Gite defeated Shiv Sena’s official candidate Narendra Darade in the Nashik local authorities election. Gite secured 357 votes in the election, defeating Darade by 109 votes and comfortably crossing the winning quota of 303.

Article Title: DVAC conducts searches at 14 locations linked to former minister EV Velu in highways corruption case; FIR registered
Article Text:
Former minister E V Velu





CHENNAI: The Directorate of Vigilance and Anti-Corruption (DVAC) conducted searches at 14 locations, including the residences and offices of former Tamil Nadu minister and Tiruvannamalai MLA E.V. Velu, on Thursday after registering a corruption case against him and 10 others over alleged irregularities in the execution of Highways Department road works in 2022.The searches began at about 7 am and were carried out by seven DVAC teams comprising 35 officials. The agency did not disclose details of the materials seized during the operation.The case was registered as DVAC Headquarters Crime No. 3 of 2026 after the State Govt granted sanction under Section 17A of the Prevention of Corruption Act. The FIR was based on a complaint submitted on April 20, 2022, by Arappor Iyakkam convener Jayaram Venkatesan, alleging large-scale misappropriation of public funds in the Highways Department.Besides E.V. Velu, the FIR named 10 other accused, including serving and retired Highways Department engineers and officials, as well as Karur-based contractor Sankaranand Infra. The case also included unknown officials of the Highways Department and private individuals.According to the FIR, the accused entered into a criminal conspiracy in 2022 to award road improvement contracts in Karur district and release payments without executing the works. Investigators alleged that Sankaranand Infra was awarded contracts worth about Rs 7 crore, of which ₹3.23 crore was paid on March 25 and March 28, 2022, despite the works not being carried out.The complaint stated that the payments related to four road improvement projects in Karur district. It further alleged that after the irregularities came to light, roads were laid in April 2022 in an attempt to erase evidence that payments had already been released in March without execution of the works.The FIR also referred to another claimed irregularity involving about Rs 1.5 crore paid under NABARD and Rural Roads projects in Karur subdivision without the execution of the sanctioned works. It stated that five officials connected with those projects were subsequently placed under suspension.The complaint further stated that similar irregularities occurred in other divisions of the State Highways Department, including projects in the Kanchipuram division, where payments of about Rs 25 crore were reportedly made without the corresponding road works being completed. It urged the DVAC to seize official records, halt further work on projects for which payments had already been released, conduct an independent audit, blacklist the contractor pending investigation, and initiate criminal proceedings against the officials and contractors involved.The DVAC stated in the FIR that the available material disclosed a prima facie case that the accused conspired to misappropriate public funds by releasing payments for road projects that were not executed. The agency alleged that the public servants dishonestly discharged their official duties by accepting undue advantage from the contractor, resulting in wrongful loss to the State exchequer.The case has been registered against the public servants under Sections 120B, 409, 468 and 471 read with Section 109 of the Indian Penal Code, along with Sections 7(c) and 13(2) read with Section 13(1)(a) of the Prevention of Corruption Act, 1988, as amended in 2018. The contractor has been booked under Sections 120B, 420, 409, 468 and 471 of the IPC, along with the relevant provisions of the Prevention of Corruption Act.
Article Summary: The Directorate of Vigilance and Anti-Corruption (DVAC) conducted searches at 14 locations, including the residences and offices of former Tamil Nadu minister E.V. Velu. The searches began at about 7 am and were carried out by seven DVAC teams comprising 35 officials. The agency did not disclose details of the materials seized during the operation.

Article Title: Sengottaiyan flays previous DMK govt for not having pursued legal remedies on Mekedatu dam issue
Article Text:
K A Sengottaiyan





ERODE: Tamil Nadu revenue minister K A Sengottaiyan on Saturday criticized the previous DMK govt for not having pursued legal remedies to stop Karnataka’s move to build a dam across the Cauvery river in Mekedatu.Speaking at a consultation meeting of officials from different departments held at the Erode district collectorate, Sengottaiyan reiterated that chief minister C Joseph Vijay had urged Prime Minister Narendra Modi to halt the move to build a dam in Mekedatu.During the review meeting, the minister told officials to deliver results quickly, saying the govt’s performance since coming to power warranted a thorough review and that the people’s demands should be met without delay. “Officers must complete necessary office procedures promptly and ensure citizens receive timely relief,” he told reporters after the meeting.The review, chaired by district collector S Kandasamy and also attended by handloom minister M Vijay Balaji, covered a wide range of administrative concerns.A major focus was the Athikadavu-Avinashi integrated drinking water project. Officials were directed to find out why more than 70 ponds were not getting filled and asked them to take remedial measures. Officials were instructed to begin silt-removal operations in the Bhavanisagar Dam to improve storage capacity and downstream supply.On allegations that liquor outlets were operating in violation of regulations, the revenue minister demanded concrete statistics and warned that strict action would follow where breaches were confirmed.
Article Summary: K A Sengottaiyan criticized the previous DMK govt for not having pursued legal remedies to stop Karnataka’s move to build a dam across the Cauvery river in Mekedatu. He reiterated that chief minister C Joseph Vijay had urged Prime Minister Narendra Modi to halt the move.

Article Title: Kanyakumari man duped of Rs11.12 lakh in fake dealership scam; 2 held from West Bengal
Article Text:
A 28-year-old man from Kanyakumari district was cheated of Rs11.12 lakh by fraudsters who promised him an electric bike dealership through a fake website.

Kanyakumari

:





A 28-year-old man from Kanyakumari district was cheated of Rs11.12 lakh by fraudsters who promised him an electric bike dealership through a fake website.Police said Sundar , from Aralvaimozhi, had applied for a dealership on a website resembling an official portal. Soon after, unidentified people contacted him via WhatsApp , assuring him of securing the dealership in Kanyakumari district. Citing various charges, including registration fees, no-objection certificate (NOC) charges, and license fees, they persuaded him to transfer money in multiple installments.Realising he had been duped and no one had contacted him thereafter, Sundar lodged a complaint with the cybercrime police.Acting on the complaint, a case was registered under the orders of Kanyakumari district superintendent of police Dr R Stalin. A special team led by Inspector Parvathi traced the money trail and bank account details.Based on the investigation, a team headed by sub-inspector Ajmal Jenif travelled to West Bengal and arrested Banti Mondal, 28, and Ujjal Karmakar, 30, on a transit warrant. They were brought to Nagercoil on Tuesday for further investigation.The accused were produced before court and remanded in judicial custody the same day.Further investigation is under way to identify other members of the racket.
Article Summary: Police said Sundar, from Aralvaimozhi, had applied for a dealership on a website resembling an official portal. Unidentified people contacted him via WhatsApp assuring him of securing the dealership in Kanyakumari district. They persuaded him to transfer money in multiple installments.

Article Title: Madurai youth’s organs save four lives
Article Text:
Madurai

:





The organs of a 28-year-old youth who died following a road accident were donated, helping save the lives of four people.Muralikannan from Madurai district was riding a two-wheeler when he rammed into the Palanganatham bridge wall around 5.10pm on Monday. He suffered a severe head injury and was admitted to the Government Rajaji Hospital (GRH), Madurai, in an unconscious state. Despite treatment by the neurosurgery and intensive care teams, Muralikannan was declared brain dead at 5.15am on Wednesday. His parents consented to organ donation , following which the harvesting procedure was carried out at the trauma care centre.His liver was donated to Apollo Hospital, Madurai, while one kidney was transplanted at Government Mahatma Gandhi Memorial Hospital and the other at Dr Murugan Hospital in Trichy. His corneas were donated to GRH, Madurai.After postmortem examination at GRH, the body was handed over to the relatives through the police department. Hospital authorities paid homage to the deceased, while the Madurai district administration accorded state honours.
Article Summary: Muralikannan from Madurai district was riding a two-wheeler when he rammed into the Palanganatham bridge wall around 5.10pm on Monday. He suffered a severe head injury and was admitted to the Government Rajaji Hospital (GRH), Madurai, in an unconscious state. Despite treatment by the neurosurgery and intensive care teams, he was declared brain dead at 5.15am on Wednesday.

Article Title: Plea in Kerala HC seeks probe into sacred ornaments missing from 20 temples
Article Text:
Gold

Kochi

:





A petition has been filed before high court seeking a court-monitored SIT probe into the alleged misappropriation of thiruvabharanams (sacred gold and silver ornaments) from nearly 20 temples under the Neyyattinkara sub-group of Travancore Devaswom Board (TDB), as revealed in the inspection report of the thiruvabharanam commissioner.The bench of Justices V Raja Vijayaraghavan and K V Jayakumar sought instructions from the state govt and TDB regarding the allegations raised in the petition filed by Akhil A S of Vellarada in Thiruvananthapuram and adjourned the matter to July 27. According to the petitioner, the inspection report submitted to the assistant commissioner of the sub-group unearthed massive discrepancies, revealing that vast quantities of invaluable temple ornaments offered to the deities by devotees over several decades are missing. Despite the commissioner having intimated the jurisdictional police authorities about the alleged theft, the petitioner contended that there had been deliberate inaction on their part.Accordingly, he approached HC seeking a directive to the home secretary and state police chief to constitute an SIT to investigate the allegations in the report. The petitioner also sought a direction that the SIT be required to file periodic reports before court to enable it to monitor the probe’s progress.
Article Summary: A petition has been filed seeking a court-monitored SIT probe into the alleged misappropriation of thiruvabharanams from nearly 20 temples. The bench of Justices V Raja Vijayaraghavan and K V Jayakumar sought instructions from the state govt and TDB regarding the allegations raised in the petition.
